mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP expands corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 9 percent signals labor market stabilization, reducing recession risk premiums and lifting equity valuations within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 50 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 9 percent over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and retail sales revenue confirm broad-based consumer demand recovery, which sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals produce negative SPX returns in more than 40 percent of observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate environment, further reductions in the federal funds rate compress discount rates and immediately boost equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no statistical difference between periods with FEDFUNDS at 0.25 percent versus periods with FEDFUNDS above 2 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.5 percent month-over-month indicates supply-side capacity utilization recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly INDPRO increases above 1.5 percent are followed by negative SPX returns in more than 55 percent of cases over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals improving labor market fundamentals, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following consecutive months of payroll growth are not significantly positive with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of low federal funds rates and declining unemployment compresses credit spreads and equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of low rates and falling unemployment fails to predict positive SPX returns in more than 50 percent of instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation exceeding 0.5 percent triggers market expectations of imminent monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 60 percent of instances following monthly CPI increases above 0.5 percent over a 3-year sample.
